chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ أَسْخَطَ الْخَالِقَ فِي مَرْضَاةِ الْمَخْلُوقِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 31 – The one who Angers the Creator for pleasing the creatures

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/411)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/411)
text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) مَنْ أَرْضَى سُلْطَاناً بِسَخَطِ اللَّهِ خَرَجَ عَنْ دِينِ الْإِسْلَامِ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Al Nowfaly, from Al Sakuny,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘The one who pleases an authority with something by Angering Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, would exit from the Religion of Al-Islam’.
en.sarwar: 2. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from al-Nawfaliy from al-Sakuniy who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, has said, Tf one pleases a Sultan (king) to displease Allah, he has gone out of al-Islam.’”
